   329вҶ’
   330вҶ’### 5.6 IBM Maximo Integration - SAFE Approach
   331вҶ’
   332вҶ’**Critical Principle:** NEVER create work orders automatically. Always human-supervised.
   333вҶ’
   334вҶ’**Integration Levels:**
   335вҶ’
   336вҶ’| Level                     | Method                             | Risk   | Control                     |
   337вҶ’| ------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------ | --------------------------- |
   338вҶ’| **Level 1: View Only**    | Display Maximo data in MMS         | Zero   | Read-only                   |
   339вҶ’| **Level 2: Export File**  | Generate CSV/XML for manual import | Low    | Human reviews before import |
   340вҶ’| **Level 3: Staging Area** | Push to Maximo "draft" status      | Medium | Human approves in Maximo    |
   341вҶ’| **Level 4: Auto-Create**  | Direct work order creation         | High   | NOT RECOMMENDED for Phase 1 |
   342вҶ’
   343вҶ’**For Demo/Phase 1 - Level 2 Only:**
   344вҶ’
   345вҶ’```
   346вҶ’[Generate Schedule] вҶ’ [Review in Calendar] вҶ’ [Approve in System]
   347вҶ’        вҶ“
   348вҶ’[Download Maximo File] вҶ’ [Review CSV Contents] вҶ’ [Import to Maximo Manually]
   349вҶ’        вҶ“
   350вҶ’[Confirm Import Success] вҶ’ [Mark as Executed in MMS]
   351вҶ’```
   352вҶ’
   353вҶ’**Maximo CSV Format:**
   354вҶ’
   355вҶ’Based on [IBM Maximo documentation](https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/csv-file-format-work-order-data):
   356вҶ’
   357вҶ’```csv
   358вҶ’SITEID,WONUM,DESCRIPTION,ASSETNUM,WORKTYPE,SCHEDSTART,ESTDUR,ESTLABHRS,STATUS
   359вҶ’DUBAI,WO-2025-001,"A-Check ALT-001",ALT-001,PM,2025-03-15,4,8,WAPPR
   360вҶ’DUBAI,WO-2025-002,"B-Check ALT-002",ALT-002,PM,2025-03-22,16,32,WAPPR
   361вҶ’```
   362вҶ’
   363вҶ’**Pre-Download Checklist (shown in UI):**
   364вҶ’
   365вҶ’- [ ] I have reviewed the schedule in the calendar view
   366вҶ’- [ ] I have validated the workload distribution
   367вҶ’- [ ] I understand these will be imported as draft work orders
   368вҶ’- [ ] I have authorization to create work orders in Maximo
   369вҶ’
   370вҶ’**Trust Message:** "You control what goes into Maximo. We generate the file; you decide when and how to import it."
   371вҶ’
   372вҶ’### 5.7 Audit Trail
   373вҶ’
   374вҶ’**Every action logged:**
   375вҶ’
   376вҶ’| Event                  | Data Captured                       |
   377вҶ’| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
   378вҶ’| Scenario created       | User, timestamp, parameters         |
   379вҶ’| Optimization run       | Input hash, output hash, solve time |
   380вҶ’| Results viewed         | User, timestamp                     |

   385вҶ’**Trust Message:** "Complete traceability. You can always prove who approved what, and when."

   426вҶ’## 7. MESSAGING FRAMEWORK
   427вҶ’
   428вҶ’### 7.1 Key Messages (Repeat Often)
   429вҶ’
   430вҶ’1. **"We specialize in exactly this problem"** - Not generic IT, not learning on your dime
   431вҶ’2. **"128 trains, 12 years, under 3 seconds"** - Proof of scale
   432вҶ’3. **"Every major metro has done this"** - Social proof, inevitability
   433вҶ’4. **"ROI in the first year"** - Financial justification (actually first MONTH based on calculations)
   434вҶ’5. **"Working software each phase"** - Risk mitigation
   435вҶ’6. **"You control the integration"** - Safety, no surprises
   436вҶ’
   437вҶ’### 7.2 Reframing Price Objection
   438вҶ’
   439вҶ’**Wrong:** "We're affordable"
   440вҶ’**Right:** "Deutsche Bahn saved EUR 74 million per year with similar optimization. Our fee is recovered in the first month of operation based on industry benchmarks."
   441вҶ’
   442вҶ’**Wrong:** "It's worth the investment"
   443вҶ’**Right:** "McKinsey estimates 15-25% maintenance cost reduction. For your fleet size and budget, that's millions per year. Our fee is a rounding error."
   444вҶ’
   445вҶ’### 7.3 Addressing Doubts
   446вҶ’
   447вҶ’| Their Fear                    | Our Response                                                                                         |
   448вҶ’| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   449вҶ’| "Too expensive"               | "DB saves EUR 74M/year. McKinsey says 15-25% reduction. What's the cost of another year with Excel?" |
   450вҶ’| "Unknown vendor"              | "You work directly with the person who builds it. No layers, no miscommunication."                   |
   451вҶ’| "Can you handle our scale?"   | "Watch this. [Run live demo with 128 trains in 3 seconds]"                                           |
   452вҶ’| "What if it doesn't work?"    | "Each phase delivers working software you own. You can stop anytime."                                |
   453вҶ’| "How do we validate results?" | "Calendar view, Excel export, PDF reports. Your team verifies everything before integration."        |
   454вҶ’| "Maximo integration is risky" | "You download a file, review it, import manually. You control every step."                           |
   455вҶ’
   456вҶ’---
   457вҶ’
   458вҶ’## 8. DARK ARTS: Advanced Persuasion Techniques
   459вҶ’
   460вҶ’> Use ethically. These are legitimate marketing/sales techniques, not manipulation.
   461вҶ’
   462вҶ’### 8.1 The Contrast Principle
   463вҶ’
   464вҶ’Show the chaotic "before" state first (peaks, violations, inefficiency). Make it look painful. THEN show the optimized state. The contrast amplifies perceived value.
   465вҶ’
   466вҶ’### 8.2 The Ben Franklin Effect
   467вҶ’
   468вҶ’Get them to do small tasks (click buttons, adjust sliders). People value things they participated in creating. The demo should be interactive, not passive.
   469вҶ’
   470вҶ’### 8.3 The Endowment Effect
   471вҶ’
   472вҶ’If possible, let them "play" with the system. Once they've invested time exploring, they'll value it more and fear losing access.
   473вҶ’
   474вҶ’### 8.4 Commitment Escalation
   475вҶ’
   476вҶ’Start with undeniable statements:
   477вҶ’
   478вҶ’- "You manage 128 trains" вҶ’ Yes
   479вҶ’- "Maintenance scheduling is complex" вҶ’ Yes
   480вҶ’- "Excel has limitations" вҶ’ Yes
   481вҶ’- "Automation would help" вҶ’ Yes
   482вҶ’- "Our system does exactly that" вҶ’ [They're primed to agree]
   483вҶ’
   484вҶ’### 8.5 The Halo Effect
   485вҶ’
   486вҶ’If the demo looks premium and polished, they'll assume the underlying code is also high quality. Never underestimate visual design.
   487вҶ’
   488вҶ’### 8.6 Urgency Without Pressure
   489вҶ’
   490вҶ’Don't create fake deadlines. Instead:
   491вҶ’
   492вҶ’- "Other metros have already implemented this"
   493вҶ’- "Every week of manual planning is X man-hours wasted"
   494вҶ’- "RTA is already investing in rail automation (ARIIS) - mileage management is next"
   495вҶ’
   496вҶ’### 8.7 The Specificity Principle
   497вҶ’
   498вҶ’Specific numbers are more believable than round numbers:
   499вҶ’
   500вҶ’- "EUR 74 million" (not "tens of millions")
   501вҶ’- "2.7 seconds solve time" (not "under 3 seconds")
   502вҶ’- "99.9% on-time" (not "nearly 100%")
   503вҶ’
   504вҶ’### 8.8 Future Pacing
   505вҶ’
   506вҶ’Help them visualize success:
   507вҶ’
   508вҶ’- "Imagine presenting this to your board next quarter..."
   509вҶ’- "When you export the first real schedule..."
   510вҶ’- "Your operations team will see the calendar and immediately understand..."
   511вҶ’

   514вҶ’## 9. TECHNICAL EXECUTION
   515вҶ’
   516вҶ’### 9.1 Demo Environment
   517вҶ’
   518вҶ’- **URL**: Must be public, memorable, professional
   519вҶ’- **Performance**: Page load <2s, optimization <3s
   520вҶ’- **Reliability**: Test extensively, have backup plan
   521вҶ’- **Mobile**: Must work (they may share link on phone)
   522вҶ’
   523вҶ’### 9.2 Data Realism
   524вҶ’
   525вҶ’| Element           | Requirement                                   |
   526вҶ’| ----------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
   527вҶ’| Fleet composition | 79 Alstom + 49 Kinki (exact)                  |
   528вҶ’| Train IDs         | ALT-001 to ALT-079, KNK-001 to KNK-049        |
   529вҶ’| Maintenance types | A-Check, B-Check, C-Check, D-Overhaul         |
   530вҶ’| Intervals         | Based on industry standards                   |
   531вҶ’| Horizon           | 52 weeks for demo, mention 12-year capability |
   532вҶ’
   533вҶ’### 9.3 What NOT to Show
   534вҶ’
   535вҶ’- Database errors or loading failures
   536вҶ’- Technical jargon (MIP, HiGHS, solver)
   537вҶ’- Incomplete features without "Coming Soon" badge

   544вҶ’## 10. POST-DEMO MATERIALS
   545вҶ’
   546вҶ’### 10.1 Leave-Behind Assets
   547вҶ’
   548вҶ’The evaluator needs ammunition for internal discussions:
   549вҶ’
   550вҶ’1. **One-pager PDF**: Problem вҶ’ Solution вҶ’ ROI вҶ’ Price

   556вҶ’### 10.2 Follow-up Strategy

   611вҶ’
   612вҶ’### Materials

   615вҶ’- [ ] Revised proposal ready
   616вҶ’- [ ] Demo recording as backup
   617вҶ’- [ ] Follow-up email drafted
   618вҶ’
   619вҶ’### Mindset
   620вҶ’
   621вҶ’- [ ] Know the key messages by heart
   622вҶ’- [ ] Memorized: DB EUR 74M, McKinsey 15-25%, MTR 50% efficiency
   623вҶ’- [ ] Prepared for common objections
   624вҶ’- [ ] Confident but not arrogant

   629вҶ’## 13. FINAL REMINDER
   630вҶ’
   631вҶ’> **The goal is not to show a demo. The goal is to WIN.**
   632вҶ’>

   634вҶ’>

   638вҶ’>
   639вҶ’> "They showed me a working system that optimized our 128 trains in 3 seconds. Deutsche Bahn saves EUR 74 million per year with similar technology. McKinsey says 15-25% cost reduction is standard. The investment pays for itself in the first month. And I can validate every result before it touches Maximo."

# DEMO STRATEGY: Win the Dubai Metro Contract

**Date:** December 9, 2025
**Status:** CRITICAL - Reputation Recovery Mode
**Objective:** Transform client perception and WIN the contract
**Mindset:** This demo must make the decision-maker CERTAIN that approving this will make their superiors proud.

---

## 1. SITUATIONAL AWARENESS

### What Happened

- Our SoW/presentation **confused the client**
- They have doubts about what exactly they're buying
- The price ($147,500) exceeds their initial expectations
- They need **internal approval/budget authorization** to proceed
- Despite confusion, **we are seen as the company that best understands the problem**

### Root Causes of Confusion (from SoW Analysis)

Our proposal used terminology that created wrong impressions:

| Term We Used                         | What They Heard                            | What We Meant                                                    |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| "Phase 0"                            | Extra phase not in RFQ, paying for nothing | First 3 weeks of Phase 1 (discovery + validation)                |
| "Optimization Playground"            | Toy that gets thrown away                  | The core optimization engine demo                                |
| "Synthetic Data"                     | Can't handle real complexity               | Stress-testing without needing their sensitive data              |
| "Source code not included" (Phase 0) | We keep everything, they get nothing       | Standard IP protection for demo; full code delivered in Phase 1+ |
| "Not to Exceed (NTE)"                | Unclear pricing model                      | Guaranteed maximum - actual price will be LOWER                  |

### The Question That Revealed Everything

> **Client asked:** "Have you done this before?"

This tells us:

- They have budget available (~$147k is acceptable range)
- They fear hiring a company that can't deliver
- They need **PROOF**, not promises
- Our SoW was too abstract - they need to SEE it working

### The Real Challenge

The person evaluating this demo is NOT the final decision-maker. They need to:

1. Understand the value proposition clearly
2. Build a case for their superiors
3. Justify the cost with concrete ROI evidence
4. Feel confident that choosing us won't backfire on their career

### Our Advantage

> "They think we understand the problem better than competitors"

This is GOLD. We must reinforce this perception while eliminating doubts about cost/value.

### The Critical Question: "Have You Done This Before?"

**This question was already asked. We MUST have a bulletproof answer.**

**The Answer (memorize this):**

> "Yes. The mathematical problem you're solving - scheduling with resource constraints over long horizons - is exactly what I worked on at MercadoLibre for logistics optimization. We scheduled millions of deliveries across constraints like vehicle capacity, time windows, and driver availability.
>
> Your problem - scheduling train maintenance across depot capacity, man-hours, and mileage limits - uses the **identical mathematical approach**. The specific constraints change (tracks instead of roads, man-hours instead of driver shifts), but the optimization technique is proven.
>
> More importantly: this exact approach is what Deutsche Bahn uses (saving EUR 74 million/year), what Hong Kong MTR uses (99.9% on-time), and what McKinsey recommends for every rail operator.
>
> I'm not experimenting with your fleet. I'm applying proven methods to your specific constraints. And the demo I'm about to show you? It runs YOUR 128 trains in under 3 seconds."

**Key elements of this answer:**

1. **Direct experience** with the same CLASS of problem
2. **Industry validation** - major metros use this
3. **Proof available now** - the demo demonstrates it
4. **Confidence without arrogance** - "proven methods" not "I'm the best"

---

## 2. STRATEGIC IMPERATIVES

### 2.1 The Psychology of Persuasion

**MEMORIZE THESE PRINCIPLES:**

| Principle         | Application                                                                           |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Anchoring**     | Show industry benchmarks FIRST (metros saving millions), then our price seems trivial |
| **Social Proof**  | Reference real metro systems (Singapore, London, Hong Kong) with documented savings   |
| **Loss Aversion** | Emphasize what they LOSE by not optimizing (overruns, violations, wasted man-hours)   |
| **Authority**     | Position ourselves as experts who've solved this exact problem class before           |
| **Scarcity**      | Imply that skilled teams for this niche problem are rare                              |
| **Commitment**    | Get small "yes" moments throughout the demo (interactive elements)                    |
| **Reciprocity**   | Give value upfront (working demo, free insights about their data)                     |

### 2.2 The "Heavier Calculator" Effect

> Research shows calculators that physically weigh more are perceived as more accurate and valuable, even with identical functionality.

**Application to our demo:**

- Show MORE metrics, not fewer
- Fill dashboards with numbers, charts, indicators
- Display all buttons (even if partial functionality)
- Use premium animations (not spinners)
- Show processing steps (even if fast) to convey complexity

**Rule:** If it looks simple, they'll think it IS simple and not worth the price.

### 2.3 The "Show, Don't Tell" Imperative

| DON'T                             | DO                                                  |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| "Our solver optimizes schedules"  | Show before/after with dramatic improvement         |
| "We handle 128 trains"            | Run live optimization with 128 trains in <3 seconds |
| "Resource leveling reduces peaks" | Animate the workload chart flattening in real-time  |
| "Industry best practices"         | Show specific case studies with dollar savings      |

---

## 3. THE DEMO MUST ACHIEVE

### 3.1 For the Evaluator

- [ ] Crystal clear understanding of what the system does
- [ ] Confidence that it works (live demo, not mockups)
- [ ] Evidence of ROI from similar implementations
- [ ] Zero technical jargon that could confuse stakeholders
- [ ] A "wow moment" they can describe to their boss

### 3.2 For Their Superiors (via the Evaluator)

- [ ] Credible ROI projection with references
- [ ] Risk mitigation (phased delivery, working software each phase)
- [ ] Industry validation (other metros have done this)
- [ ] Clear deliverables per phase
- [ ] Pride-worthy decision ("We found an expert team at a good price")

### 3.3 The Ultimate Test

> After the demo, the evaluator must be able to say:
>
> "I found a company that specializes in exactly our problem. They showed me a working system that optimized our 128 trains in seconds. Other metros have saved millions with this approach. The cost is X, and based on industry benchmarks, we'll recover it in the first year through efficiency gains."

---

## 4. ROI EVIDENCE - INDUSTRY DATA

### 4.1 Documented Case Studies (Use in Demo)

| Organization            | Savings/Results                                                 | Source                                                                                                                                             |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Deutsche Bahn**       | **EUR 74 million/year** + 34,000 tons CO2 reduction             | [INFORMS Journal](https://pubsonline.informs.org/doi/10.1287/inte.2020.1069)                                                                       |
| **Deutsche Bahn**       | **25% cost reduction** through predictive maintenance           | [KONUX Partnership](https://www.konux.com/)                                                                                                        |
| **Hong Kong MTR**       | **50% improvement** in maintenance efficiency, 99.9% on-time    | [AAAI Award](https://cdn.aaai.org/AAAI/2005/IAAI05-003.pdf)                                                                                        |
| **London Underground**  | **GBP 3 million/year** from Central line motor prediction alone | [Computerworld](https://www.computerworld.com/article/1655414/)                                                                                    |
| **SMRT Singapore**      | **>1 million km** between failures (MKBF benchmark)             | [Bentley Systems](https://investors.bentley.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bentley-systems-and-smrt-trains-collaborate-improve-safety-and) |
| **Netherlands ProRail** | **Two-thirds decrease** in costs vs human scheduler             | [Wiley Research](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/mice.12764)                                                                           |
| **Keolis Bordeaux**     | **10% increase** in fleet availability                          | [Keolis Blog](https://blog.keolisna.com/lean-management-systems-for-maintenance-of-transit-assets-rolling-stock-and-infrastructure)                |

### 4.2 Industry-Wide Statistics

From [McKinsey Rail Maintenance Report](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/travel-logistics-and-infrastructure/our-insights/the-rail-sectors-changing-maintenance-game):

> "With 10 to 15 percent efficiency gains expected, the global rail maintenance market can save up to around **EUR 7.5 billion per year** by moving toward condition-based maintenance."

> "Integrating condition-based and predictive maintenance throughout the rail sector would reduce maintenance costs by **15-25 percent**."

> "Approximately **30% of workshop time** is taken up by manual failure diagnostics - time that can be eliminated with proper systems."

From [WorkTrek Predictive Maintenance Analysis](https://worktrek.com/blog/predictive-maintenance-cost-savings/):

> "Organizations experience **30-50% reduction in unplanned downtime**. **95% of organizations** implementing predictive maintenance report positive returns, with **27% achieving full payback within 12 months**."

> "Leading organizations achieve **10:1 to 30:1 ROI ratios** within 12-18 months of implementation."

From [VLink AI in Railways](https://vlinkinfo.com/blog/ai-in-railways):

> "Predictive maintenance reduces overall maintenance costs by **18-25%** compared to traditional approaches while delivering **8-12% savings** over preventive maintenance alone."

### 4.3 Dubai-Specific Context

From our research document:

> **Current state:** "Manual mileage management via Excel. IBM Maximo for asset management (not mileage-specific)."

> **Industry movement:** RTA already invested in ARIIS (Automated Rail Infrastructure Inspection System) expecting to "reduce manual inspections by 70%, improve infrastructure assessment by 40%, and reduce inspection times by 75%."

**Message to client:** Dubai is already investing in rail automation. Mileage management is the obvious next step.

### 4.4 ROI Calculation for Dubai Metro

**Conservative Projection (for demo):**

```
Fleet: 128 trains
Annual maintenance budget (estimate): $15-20 million

Conservative savings (15% - McKinsey low end):
  $15M x 15% = $2.25 million/year

Our fee: $147,500 (one-time)

Payback period: < 1 month of savings
5-year ROI: 76x ($11.25M savings / $147.5K investment)
```

**Key message:** "Based on industry benchmarks, the investment pays for itself in the first month of operation."

---

## 5. VALIDATION SYSTEM - HOW THEY VERIFY RESULTS

### 5.1 The Validation Challenge

The client needs to:

1. **Trust** that the optimization is correct
2. **Verify** that scheduled dates make sense
3. **Share** results with colleagues for review
4. **Integrate** with existing systems (Maximo) safely

### 5.2 Multi-Level Validation Architecture

```
Level 1: VISUAL VALIDATION (Dashboard)
         вҶ“
         Quick metrics, before/after comparison
         "Does this look reasonable?"

Level 2: DETAILED EXPLORATION (Calendar)
         вҶ“
         Click on any train, any week, see details
         "Why is ALT-015 scheduled for week 12?"

Level 3: EXPORT FOR OFFLINE REVIEW (Excel/PDF)
         вҶ“
         Share via email, print for meetings
         "Let me show this to the maintenance team"

Level 4: APPROVAL WORKFLOW (System)
         вҶ“
         Explicit "Approve Scenario" button
         "I confirm this schedule is correct"

Level 5: SAFE INTEGRATION (Maximo Export)
         вҶ“
         Generate import file, human reviews, manual import
         "I control what goes into Maximo"
```

### 5.3 Calendar View (Google Calendar Style)

**Implementation Requirements:**

| Feature                       | Purpose                   | Trust Builder                  |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| Monthly/Weekly/Daily views    | Familiar interface        | "I know how to use this"       |
| Color by maintenance type     | Quick visual scan         | "I can spot patterns"          |
| Color by fleet (Alstom/Kinki) | Fleet-specific validation | "My fleet looks right"         |
| Click for details             | Drill-down capability     | "I can verify any entry"       |
| Filter by train/type/depot    | Focus on areas of concern | "Show me only D-Overhauls"     |
| Conflict highlighting         | Automatic issue detection | "System found problems for me" |
| Week-over-week workload bar   | Resource validation       | "Workload looks balanced"      |

**Validation Questions the Calendar Answers:**

- "Is train ALT-015 really due for maintenance in week 12?"
- "Are there too many D-Overhauls in March?"
- "Does the Kinki fleet schedule align with our depot capacity?"

### 5.4 Excel Export (Complete Data Package)

**Workbook Structure:**

| Tab                   | Content                                          | Purpose                     |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------- |
| **Executive Summary** | KPIs, before/after metrics, charts               | For management presentation |
| **Full Schedule**     | Every event: Train, Type, Week, Man-Hours, Depot | Complete validation         |
| **Weekly Workload**   | Man-hours per week, capacity utilization         | Resource planning           |
| **Train Summary**     | Each train's scheduled maintenance               | Per-asset verification      |
| **Compliance Report** | On-time %, tolerance violations                  | Regulatory evidence         |
| **Cost Projection**   | Estimated costs by period                        | Budget planning             |

**Excel Features:**

- Conditional formatting (red for violations, green for compliant)
- Pivot table ready (filter by any dimension)
- Charts embedded (workload profile, compliance rate)
- Print-ready formatting
- Clear headers and legends

**Trust Message:** "This is YOUR data. Analyze it however you want. Share it with whoever needs to see it."

### 5.5 PDF Report (Board-Ready Document)

**Contents:**

1. **Cover Page**
   - "Dubai Metro Maintenance Schedule - Optimized"
   - Date generated
   - Scenario name
   - System version

2. **Executive Summary (1 page)**
   - Fleet size: 128 trains
   - Horizon: 52 weeks
   - Peak reduction: X%
   - Compliance rate: X%
   - Estimated savings: $X

3. **Before/After Comparison (1 page)**
   - Side-by-side workload charts
   - Key metrics comparison table

4. **Monthly Breakdown (12 pages)**
   - One page per month
   - Calendar view
   - Workload chart
   - Event list

5. **Appendix**
   - Methodology notes
   - Data sources
   - Approval signature line (blank)

**Trust Message:** "Print this. Present it to your board. Get approval."

### 5.6 IBM Maximo Integration - SAFE Approach

**Critical Principle:** NEVER create work orders automatically. Always human-supervised.

**Integration Levels:**

| Level                     | Method                             | Risk   | Control                     |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------ | --------------------------- |
| **Level 1: View Only**    | Display Maximo data in MMS         | Zero   | Read-only                   |
| **Level 2: Export File**  | Generate CSV/XML for manual import | Low    | Human reviews before import |
| **Level 3: Staging Area** | Push to Maximo "draft" status      | Medium | Human approves in Maximo    |
| **Level 4: Auto-Create**  | Direct work order creation         | High   | NOT RECOMMENDED for Phase 1 |

**For Demo/Phase 1 - Level 2 Only:**

```
[Generate Schedule] вҶ’ [Review in Calendar] вҶ’ [Approve in System]
        вҶ“
[Download Maximo File] вҶ’ [Review CSV Contents] вҶ’ [Import to Maximo Manually]
        вҶ“
[Confirm Import Success] вҶ’ [Mark as Executed in MMS]
```

**Maximo CSV Format:**

Based on [IBM Maximo documentation](https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/csv-file-format-work-order-data):

```csv
SITEID,WONUM,DESCRIPTION,ASSETNUM,WORKTYPE,SCHEDSTART,ESTDUR,ESTLABHRS,STATUS
DUBAI,WO-2025-001,"A-Check ALT-001",ALT-001,PM,2025-03-15,4,8,WAPPR
DUBAI,WO-2025-002,"B-Check ALT-002",ALT-002,PM,2025-03-22,16,32,WAPPR
```

**Pre-Download Checklist (shown in UI):**

- [ ] I have reviewed the schedule in the calendar view
- [ ] I have validated the workload distribution
- [ ] I understand these will be imported as draft work orders
- [ ] I have authorization to create work orders in Maximo

**Trust Message:** "You control what goes into Maximo. We generate the file; you decide when and how to import it."

### 5.7 Audit Trail

**Every action logged:**

| Event                  | Data Captured                       |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| Scenario created       | User, timestamp, parameters         |
| Optimization run       | Input hash, output hash, solve time |
| Results viewed         | User, timestamp                     |
| Export generated       | User, format, timestamp             |
| Scenario approved      | User, timestamp, approval notes     |
| Maximo file downloaded | User, timestamp, record count       |

**Trust Message:** "Complete traceability. You can always prove who approved what, and when."

---

## 6. DEMO CONTENT REQUIREMENTS

### 6.1 Must-Have Screens

| Screen                | Purpose                       | Wow Factor                                |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| **Dashboard**         | Overview of fleet health      | Sparklines, KPIs, color-coded status      |
| **Fleet View**        | 128 trains with live status   | Real Alstom/Kinki distinction             |
| **What-If Simulator** | CORE DEMO - live optimization | Watch workload chart flatten in real-time |
| **Calendar View**     | Schedule validation           | Google Calendar-style, click for details  |
| **Schedule Table**    | Detailed event list           | Sortable, filterable, exportable          |
| **ROI Calculator**    | Show projected savings        | Based on their actual fleet numbers       |
| **Case Studies**      | Industry proof                | Links to credible sources                 |
| **Export Center**     | Excel, PDF, Maximo file       | Professional downloads                    |

### 6.2 Visual Standards

- **Premium feel**: No amateur design, no default Bootstrap
- **Enterprise credibility**: Cloudscape (AWS design system) conveys seriousness
- **Data density**: Fill the screen with meaningful information
- **Motion**: Smooth transitions, chart animations, progress indicators
- **Badges**: "AI-Optimized", "Coming in Phase 2", "Industry Benchmark"

### 6.3 Interactive Elements

The evaluator should be able to:

- [ ] Adjust parameters (fleet size, horizon, capacity)
- [ ] Click "Optimize" and watch it work
- [ ] Compare before/after scenarios
- [ ] Navigate calendar and click on events
- [ ] Export Excel with real data
- [ ] Download PDF report
- [ ] See Maximo file preview

---

## 7. MESSAGING FRAMEWORK

### 7.1 Key Messages (Repeat Often)

1. **"We specialize in exactly this problem"** - Not generic IT, not learning on your dime
2. **"128 trains, 12 years, under 3 seconds"** - Proof of scale
3. **"Every major metro has done this"** - Social proof, inevitability
4. **"ROI in the first year"** - Financial justification (actually first MONTH based on calculations)
5. **"Working software each phase"** - Risk mitigation
6. **"You control the integration"** - Safety, no surprises

### 7.2 Reframing Price Objection

**Wrong:** "We're affordable"
**Right:** "Deutsche Bahn saved EUR 74 million per year with similar optimization. Our fee is recovered in the first month of operation based on industry benchmarks."

**Wrong:** "It's worth the investment"
**Right:** "McKinsey estimates 15-25% maintenance cost reduction. For your fleet size and budget, that's millions per year. Our fee is a rounding error."

### 7.3 Addressing Doubts

| Their Fear                    | Our Response                                                                                         |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| "Too expensive"               | "DB saves EUR 74M/year. McKinsey says 15-25% reduction. What's the cost of another year with Excel?" |
| "Unknown vendor"              | "You work directly with the person who builds it. No layers, no miscommunication."                   |
| "Can you handle our scale?"   | "Watch this. [Run live demo with 128 trains in 3 seconds]"                                           |
| "What if it doesn't work?"    | "Each phase delivers working software you own. You can stop anytime."                                |
| "How do we validate results?" | "Calendar view, Excel export, PDF reports. Your team verifies everything before integration."        |
| "Maximo integration is risky" | "You download a file, review it, import manually. You control every step."                           |

---

## 8. DARK ARTS: Advanced Persuasion Techniques

> Use ethically. These are legitimate marketing/sales techniques, not manipulation.

### 8.1 The Contrast Principle

Show the chaotic "before" state first (peaks, violations, inefficiency). Make it look painful. THEN show the optimized state. The contrast amplifies perceived value.

### 8.2 The Ben Franklin Effect

Get them to do small tasks (click buttons, adjust sliders). People value things they participated in creating. The demo should be interactive, not passive.

### 8.3 The Endowment Effect

If possible, let them "play" with the system. Once they've invested time exploring, they'll value it more and fear losing access.

### 8.4 Commitment Escalation

Start with undeniable statements:

- "You manage 128 trains" вҶ’ Yes
- "Maintenance scheduling is complex" вҶ’ Yes
- "Excel has limitations" вҶ’ Yes
- "Automation would help" вҶ’ Yes
- "Our system does exactly that" вҶ’ [They're primed to agree]

### 8.5 The Halo Effect

If the demo looks premium and polished, they'll assume the underlying code is also high quality. Never underestimate visual design.

### 8.6 Urgency Without Pressure

Don't create fake deadlines. Instead:

- "Other metros have already implemented this"
- "Every week of manual planning is X man-hours wasted"
- "RTA is already investing in rail automation (ARIIS) - mileage management is next"

### 8.7 The Specificity Principle

Specific numbers are more believable than round numbers:

- "EUR 74 million" (not "tens of millions")
- "2.7 seconds solve time" (not "under 3 seconds")
- "99.9% on-time" (not "nearly 100%")

### 8.8 Future Pacing

Help them visualize success:

- "Imagine presenting this to your board next quarter..."
- "When you export the first real schedule..."
- "Your operations team will see the calendar and immediately understand..."

---

## 9. TECHNICAL EXECUTION

### 9.1 Demo Environment

- **URL**: Must be public, memorable, professional
- **Performance**: Page load <2s, optimization <3s
- **Reliability**: Test extensively, have backup plan
- **Mobile**: Must work (they may share link on phone)

### 9.2 Data Realism

| Element           | Requirement                                   |
| ----------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Fleet composition | 79 Alstom + 49 Kinki (exact)                  |
| Train IDs         | ALT-001 to ALT-079, KNK-001 to KNK-049        |
| Maintenance types | A-Check, B-Check, C-Check, D-Overhaul         |
| Intervals         | Based on industry standards                   |
| Horizon           | 52 weeks for demo, mention 12-year capability |

### 9.3 What NOT to Show

- Database errors or loading failures
- Technical jargon (MIP, HiGHS, solver)
- Incomplete features without "Coming Soon" badge
- Slow responses or timeouts
- Empty states or missing data
- Console errors (check before demo!)

---

## 10. POST-DEMO MATERIALS

### 10.1 Leave-Behind Assets

The evaluator needs ammunition for internal discussions:

1. **One-pager PDF**: Problem вҶ’ Solution вҶ’ ROI вҶ’ Price
2. **Case study links**: Deutsche Bahn, Hong Kong MTR, McKinsey report
3. **Demo recording**: If they can't run it again
4. **Excel sample**: Generated by the demo
5. **Revised proposal**: Clean, no confusing terms

### 10.2 Follow-up Strategy

- Send thank-you within 2 hours
- Include summary of what was demonstrated
- Attach PDF report generated during demo
- Link to case studies mentioned
- Offer to answer any questions
- Provide timeline for next steps
- Make it easy for them to say yes

---

## 11. SUCCESS METRICS

### The demo succeeded if:

- [ ] Evaluator can explain the value to their boss in one sentence
- [ ] They saw the system work with THEIR fleet parameters (128 trains)
- [ ] ROI justification is clear (EUR 74M DB example, McKinsey 15-25%)
- [ ] They understand the validation workflow (Calendar вҶ’ Excel вҶ’ Approve вҶ’ Maximo)
- [ ] They feel confident, not confused
- [ ] They have materials to share internally (PDF, Excel)
- [ ] "Wow moment" occurred (they were visibly impressed)

### The demo failed if:

- [ ] They still have questions about what it does
- [ ] They're unsure if it handles their scale
- [ ] They can't articulate ROI to superiors
- [ ] They don't understand how to validate results
- [ ] Technical issues undermined credibility
- [ ] They feel overwhelmed or confused

---

## 12. CHECKLIST BEFORE DEMO

### Technical

- [ ] Demo URL is live and fast
- [ ] All features work with 128 trains
- [ ] Calendar view loads and is interactive
- [ ] Excel export downloads correctly
- [ ] PDF export generates properly
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] Works on mobile
- [ ] Backup plan ready (local version? screen recording?)

### Content

- [ ] ROI numbers are realistic and sourced
- [ ] Case studies have credible links (McKinsey, INFORMS, AAAI)
- [ ] Fleet data matches their reality (79 Alstom, 49 Kinki)
- [ ] No technical jargon visible
- [ ] Maximo export format verified against IBM docs

### Materials

- [ ] One-pager PDF ready
- [ ] Revised proposal ready
- [ ] Demo recording as backup
- [ ] Follow-up email drafted

### Mindset

- [ ] Know the key messages by heart
- [ ] Memorized: DB EUR 74M, McKinsey 15-25%, MTR 50% efficiency
- [ ] Prepared for common objections
- [ ] Confident but not arrogant
- [ ] Ready to say "I'll research that" for unknowns

---

## 13. FINAL REMINDER

> **The goal is not to show a demo. The goal is to WIN.**
>
> Every screen, every animation, every word should move them closer to "yes."
>
> The evaluator's career is on the line. Make them feel safe choosing us.
>
> When their boss asks "Why this company?", they should have a clear, confident answer:
>
> "They showed me a working system that optimized our 128 trains in 3 seconds. Deutsche Bahn saves EUR 74 million per year with similar technology. McKinsey says 15-25% cost reduction is standard. The investment pays for itself in the first month. And I can validate every result before it touches Maximo."

---

## REFERENCES

### McKinsey Reports

- [The Rail Sector's Changing Maintenance Game](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/travel-logistics-and-infrastructure/our-insights/the-rail-sectors-changing-maintenance-game)
- [Using Analytics to Get European Rail Maintenance on Track](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/public-sector/our-insights/using-analytics-to-get-european-rail-maintenance-on-track)

### Case Studies

- [Deutsche Bahn - INFORMS](https://pubsonline.informs.org/doi/10.1287/inte.2020.1069) - EUR 74M savings
- [Hong Kong MTR - AAAI](https://cdn.aaai.org/AAAI/2005/IAAI05-003.pdf) - 50% efficiency improvement
- [London Underground - Computerworld](https://www.computerworld.com/article/1655414/) - GBP 3M/year from motor prediction
- [SMRT Singapore - Bentley](https://investors.bentley.com/news-releases/news-release-details/bentley-systems-and-smrt-trains-collaborate-improve-safety-and)
- [Netherlands ProRail - Wiley](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/mice.12764)

### IBM Maximo Integration

- [CSV File Format for Work Order Data](https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/csv-file-format-work-order-data)
- [How to Import Data into Maximo Using Excel](https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/how-import-data-maximo-using-excel-file)
- [Maximo REST API - File Import](https://ibm-maximo-dev.github.io/maximo-restapi-documentation/file/import/)

### Industry Statistics

- [WorkTrek - Predictive Maintenance Cost Savings](https://worktrek.com/blog/predictive-maintenance-cost-savings/)
- [VLink - AI in Railways](https://vlinkinfo.com/blog/ai-in-railways)
- [Appinventiv - AI in Railways](https://appinventiv.com/blog/ai-in-railways/)

### Project Internal Documents

- `docs/RESEARCH_MAINTENANCE_SCHEDULING.md` - Comprehensive research
- `docs/internal/FINAL_RECOVERY_PLAN.md` - Recovery strategy
- `docs/internal/CLIENT-CONTEXT.md` - Client information

### Companion Documents (Created Dec 9, 2025)

- **`docs/internal/DEMO-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md`** - Cronograma, priorizaГ§ГЈo, especificaГ§Гөes tГ©cnicas
- **`docs/internal/DEMO-SCRIPT.md`** - Roteiro completo da apresentaГ§ГЈo, Q&A, fallbacks

---

## APPENDIX A: FEATURE STATUS MATRIX

### What EXISTS vs What Needs IMPLEMENTATION

| Feature | Status | Location | Notes |
|---------|--------|----------|-------|
| **Python Solver** | вң… EXISTS | `packages/solver/` | 16/16 tests, <3s |
| **API Server** | вң… EXISTS | `packages/api/` | Hono + Bun |
| **What-If Simulator** | вң… EXISTS | `apps/mms/app/routes/what-if.tsx` | Core feature |
| **Workload Charts** | вң… EXISTS | `apps/mms/` | Before/after |
| **Dashboard** | вҡ пёҸ PARTIAL | `apps/mms/app/routes/dashboard.tsx` | Needs real data |
| **Fleet Page** | вҡ пёҸ PARTIAL | `apps/mms/app/routes/fleet.tsx` | Needs 79+49 trains |
| **Schedule Page** | вҡ пёҸ PARTIAL | `apps/mms/app/routes/schedule.tsx` | Needs real data |
| **Calendar View** | вқҢ BUILD | - | High priority |
| **Excel Export** | вқҢ BUILD | - | High priority |
| **PDF Report** | вқҢ BUILD | - | Medium priority |
| **ROI Calculator** | вқҢ BUILD | - | Low effort |
| **Maximo Export** | вқҢ BUILD | - | Low priority for demo |

### Priority Order for Demo

1. **P0 (MUST):** Calendar View, Excel Export, Realistic Fleet Data
2. **P1 (SHOULD):** PDF Report, Dashboard real metrics, ROI display
3. **P2 (NICE):** Case Studies page, Maximo CSV preview

See `DEMO-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md` for detailed schedule and specifications.

---

_This document consolidates strategy from DEMO-FOLLOWUP-PLAN.md, FINAL_RECOVERY_PLAN.md, new requirements from December 9, 2025, and industry research._

_Last updated: December 9, 2025_

   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’| Risk | Mitigation |
   264вҶ’|------|------------|

   268вҶ’| Deployment fails | Have local demo ready for screen recording |

   275вҶ’- **Focus on substance, not polish.** A working ugly demo > beautiful mockup

   277вҶ’- **Comparison is key.** Show "before" (bad) vs "after" (good) clearly
   278вҶ’- **Keep scope minimal.** Every feature risks the deadline

# Demo & Follow-up Plan

> **Deadline: Monday, December 1st, 2025**

---

## рҹҺҜ Objective

Create a working demo to send with the client follow-up email, demonstrating our technical capability to deliver the optimization solution.

---

## рҹ“… Timeline

| Day | Date | Focus |
|-----|------|-------|
| Fri night/Sat | Nov 29-30 | Solver service + basic optimization |
| Sat/Sun | Nov 30 - Dec 1 | Frontend + visualization |
| Sun evening | Dec 1 | Deploy + test |
| Mon morning | Dec 1 | Send follow-up email |

---

## вң… Demo Requirements (MVP)

### Must Have (for Monday)
- [ ] Python solver service running
- [ ] HiGHS solving a real MIP problem
- [ ] Sample data: 20 trains, 1 year horizon
- [ ] "Before" chart: weekly man-hours (unoptimized/FIFO)
- [ ] "After" chart: weekly man-hours (optimized)
- [ ] Key metrics displayed:
  - Peak reduction percentage
  - Solve time
  - Number of events scheduled
- [ ] Deployed to public URL

### Nice to Have (if time permits)
- [ ] Interactive controls (adjust parameters)
- [ ] Multiple scenarios
- [ ] Export/download results
- [ ] Better styling

### Explicitly NOT Needed
- Authentication
- Database
- API documentation
- Full error handling
- Mobile responsiveness
- Perfect UI

---

## рҹҸ—пёҸ Technical Approach

### Option A: Full Stack (Recommended)
```
Frontend (Astro + React)  вҶ’  API (Bun.serve)  вҶ’  Solver (Python CLI)
         вҶ“                        вҶ“
       Railway              (same process, spawns Python)
```

### Option B: Simpler (Fallback)
```
Static HTML + JS  вҶ’  Bun.serve + Python subprocess
                            вҶ“
                        Railway
```

### Option C: Simplest (Emergency)
```
Streamlit App (Python only)
         вҶ“
    Streamlit Cloud
```

**Decision:** Start with Option A. Fall back to B or C if blocked.

---

## рҹ“Ҡ Sample Data Specification

```python
# 20 trains, 2 fleets
trains = [
    {"id": f"ALT-{i:02d}", "fleet": "Alstom", "current_km": random(0, 40000)}
    for i in range(1, 11)
] + [
    {"id": f"KIN-{i:02d}", "fleet": "Kinki", "current_km": random(0, 35000)}
    for i in range(1, 11)
]

# Maintenance rules
rules = [
    {"id": "PM50k", "km_trigger": 50000, "duration_days": 5, "man_hours": 100},
    {"id": "PM100k", "km_trigger": 100000, "duration_days": 7, "man_hours": 150},
]

# Usage assumptions
usage = {
    "Alstom": 280,  # km/day average
    "Kinki": 320,   # km/day average
}

# Resources
resources = {
    "max_tracks_per_day": 3,
    "max_man_hours_per_week": 800,
}

# Horizon
horizon_days = 365  # 1 year for demo (faster solve)
```

---

## рҹ“§ Follow-up Email Drafts

### Version A: With Demo (Preferred)

```
Subject: MMS Proposal Follow-up - Technical Demo Available

Dear Puneet,

I hope this message finds you well.

I wanted to follow up on the Mileage Management System proposal 
we submitted on November 18th.

While awaiting your feedback, I've built a small proof-of-concept 
to validate our optimization approach:

вҶ’ [DEMO LINK]

This demo shows the core resource-leveling algorithm working on 
sample fleet data. You can see how the system reduces workload 
peaks by optimizing maintenance schedules.

Key results from the demo:
вҖў Peak weekly man-hours reduced by [X]%
вҖў [N] maintenance events scheduled optimally
вҖў Solution computed in [X] seconds

The production system would include your actual fleet configuration, 
Maximo integration, and full reporting capabilities as outlined in 
our proposal.

I'm happy to walk you through the demo or answer any questions 
about our approach.

Best regards,
Antonio Silva
```

### Version B: Without Demo (Fallback)

```
Subject: Following up - Mileage Management System Proposal

Dear Puneet,

I hope this message finds you well.

I wanted to follow up on the Mileage Management System proposal 
we submitted on November 18th for the Dubai Metro fleet.

Please let me know if you need any clarification on our approach 
or if there's additional information that would help your evaluation.

I remain very interested in this opportunity and am available to 
discuss any aspects of the proposal at your convenience.

Best regards,
Antonio Silva
```

### Version C: Hybrid (With Technical Detail but No Demo)

```
Subject: MMS Proposal Follow-up - Technical Validation Update

Dear Puneet,

I wanted to follow up on our proposal submitted on November 18th.

Since then, I've been working on technical validation of the 
optimization approach. Initial results are promising:

вҖў Successfully modeled the resource-constrained scheduling problem
вҖў HiGHS solver handles 100+ maintenance events efficiently
вҖў Resource leveling achieves significant peak reduction

I'd welcome the opportunity to discuss these findings or address 
any questions about our proposal.

Best regards,
Antonio Silva
```

---

## рҹҡҖ Deployment Options

### Frontend

| Platform | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| Vercel | Free, fast, easy | None for this use |
| Netlify | Free, fast | None for this use |
| GitHub Pages | Free | Static only |

**Recommendation:** Vercel (instant deploys, good DX)

### Backend (Bun + Python Solver)

| Platform | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| Railway | Native Bun support, Python via Nixpacks | $5/month after free tier |
| Render | Free tier available | Cold starts |
| Fly.io | Good free tier | Slightly more complex |

**Recommendation:** Railway (supports Bun natively + Python via Nixpacks)

---

## рҹ“Ӣ Implementation Checklist

### Day 1: Python Solver CLI
- [ ] Set up Python project structure in services/solver
- [ ] Install HiGHS, Pydantic
- [ ] Implement CLI that reads JSON from stdin
- [ ] Implement basic model builder
- [ ] Test with 20 trains, 1 year

### Day 2: TypeScript Integration + Comparison
- [ ] Create sample data generator in packages/engine
- [ ] Implement solver client (Bun subprocess)
- [ ] Implement "naive" FIFO scheduler (for comparison)
- [ ] Verify optimization improves over naive
- [ ] Create API endpoint for /compare

### Day 3: Frontend
- [ ] Set up Astro + React frontend
- [ ] Create basic layout
- [ ] Implement API client
- [ ] Create bar chart (weekly man-hours)
- [ ] Show before/after comparison

### Day 4: Polish & Deploy
- [ ] Display key metrics
- [ ] Basic styling
- [ ] Test full flow
- [ ] Deploy to Railway (single app: Bun + Python)
- [ ] Test production URL
- [ ] Prepare email with link
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md status

---

## вҡ пёҸ Risk Mitigation

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| HiGHS installation issues | Use Docker, or fall back to OR-Tools |
| Solver too slow | Reduce problem size (10 trains, 6 months) |
| Frontend takes too long | Use Streamlit instead |
| Deployment fails | Have local demo ready for screen recording |
| No time to finish | Send Version B email (no demo) |

---

## рҹ“қ Notes

- **Focus on substance, not polish.** A working ugly demo > beautiful mockup
- **Real optimization required.** Must use actual MIP solver, not fake data
- **Comparison is key.** Show "before" (bad) vs "after" (good) clearly
- **Keep scope minimal.** Every feature risks the deadline

# FINAL RECOVERY PLAN: Keolis MMS Project

**Date:** December 4, 2025
**Status:** Critical - Sales Recovery Mode
**Deadline:** Presentation delivery by December 11, 2025
**Objective:** Transform client perception from "risky experiment" to "proven solution"

---

## 1. SITUATION SUMMARY

### What Happened
- We submitted a SoW that confused the client
- Meeting on Dec 4 revealed their concerns
- We promised a polished presentation for next week

### The Real Problem (Post-Meeting Insight)
**The client does NOT care about price. They care about CAPABILITY.**

Key question from meeting: *"Have you done this before?"*

This tells us:
- They have budget approved (~$147k is acceptable)
- They fear hiring a company that can't deliver
- They need PROOF, not promises

### Root Causes of Confusion

| SoW Term | Client Interpretation | Reality |
|----------|----------------------|---------|
| "Phase 0" | Extra phase not in RFQ, paying for nothing | First 3 weeks of Phase 1 |
| "Playground" | Toy that gets thrown away | The core optimization engine |
| "Synthetic Data" | Can't handle real complexity | Stress-testing edge cases |
| "Source code not included" | We keep everything, they get nothing | Standard IP protection clause |
| "Maximo in Phase 3" | Manual work for months | CSV export works Day 1 |

---

## 2. STRATEGIC RESPONSE

### 2.1 Core Message Shift

**OLD (Weak):** "Pay us to explore if we can solve your problem"
**NEW (Strong):** "We've solved this problem before. Here's how we'll solve it for you."

### 2.2 Authority Positioning

When asked "Have you done this before?", the answer is:

> "This exact problem - scheduling with resource constraints over long horizons - is what I worked on at MercadoLibre for logistics routing. Mathematically, scheduling train maintenance and routing delivery trucks are the same class of problem. The constraints change (tracks instead of roads, man-hours instead of driver shifts), but the optimization technique is identical. I'm not experimenting; I'm applying proven methods to your specific constraints."

### 2.3 Phase Realignment with RFQ

The RFQ defines 3 phases. Our SoW invented Phase 0. We must realign:

| RFQ Phase | Our Delivery | What Client Gets |
|-----------|--------------|------------------|
| **Phase 1** | Mileage Management + Run Assignment | Working system with daily recommendations |
| **Phase 2** | Costing Module | Budget forecasting and TCO analysis |
| **Phase 3** | Expansion + Future | Maximo API automation, alerts |

**Critical:** The "3-week calibration" is Sprint 1-3 of Phase 1, NOT a separate phase.

### 2.4 Maximo Integration Clarity

| Level | When | How | Client Effort |
|-------|------|-----|---------------|
| **Batch (Phase 1)** | Day 1 | Export CSV вҶ’ Import to Maximo | 5 min/day |
| **API (Phase 3)** | Later | Automatic sync | Zero |

**Message:** "You can use the system with Maximo from Day 1. The API just removes the manual step."

---

## 3. THE DELIVERABLE: INTERACTIVE PRESENTATION

### 3.1 What We're Building

A web-based presentation at a public URL that:
1. **Tells our story** - Slides explaining the solution in simple terms
2. **Shows the problem** - Their actual workload charts from the RFQ
3. **Demonstrates the solution** - Live optimization running in the browser
4. **Presents the revised proposal** - Clear phases, clear deliverables, clear prices

### 3.2 Presentation Structure

```
/                    вҶ’ Hero: "Dubai Metro Mileage Management System"
/challenge           вҶ’ The Problem (their RFQ charts)
/solution            вҶ’ How We Solve It (simple explanation)
/demo                вҶ’ LIVE INTERACTIVE DEMO (the key slide)
/approach            вҶ’ Our Methodology (phases aligned to RFQ)
/experience          вҶ’ Why Us (MercadoLibre, track record)
/proposal            вҶ’ Revised Commercial Terms
/next-steps          вҶ’ Call to Action
```

### 3.3 The Demo Page (Critical)

This is what wins the deal. The client must:
1. See a "Before" chart - chaotic workload with peaks
2. Click "Optimize"
3. Watch it transform into leveled workload
4. See metrics: Peak reduction, variance reduction, zero violations

**Technical approach:**
- Use `glpk.js` (WebAssembly) for client-side optimization
- Pre-configured with realistic parameters (128 trains, 52 weeks)
- Runs in under 3 seconds
- No backend required = easy to deploy

### 3.4 Language Rules

| DO NOT SAY | SAY INSTEAD |
|------------|-------------|
| MIP, Linear Programming | "Optimization algorithm" |
| PWA | "Web application" |
| Solver | "The system" |
| Synthetic data | "Test scenarios" |
| Phase 0 | (never mention) |
| Playground | "Optimization console" or just "the system" |

---

## 4. REVISED COMMERCIAL STRUCTURE

### 4.1 Pricing (Unchanged, but restructured)

| Phase | Scope | Duration | Price | Type |
|-------|-------|----------|-------|------|
| **Phase 1** | Core System + Daily Operations | 12 weeks | **$67,500** | Fixed |
| **Phase 2** | Costing & Reporting | 6 weeks | **$35,000** | Fixed |
| **Phase 3** | Maximo API + Alerts | 8 weeks | **$45,000** | Fixed |
| **Total** | Complete System | ~26 weeks | **$147,500** | Fixed |

**Note:** Phase 1 now includes the original "Phase 0" scope. No separate line item.

### 4.2 What Each Phase Delivers

**Phase 1 - Core System ($67,500)**
- Optimization engine calibrated to their rules
- Web dashboard for planners
- Daily RUC recommendations (which trains to use)
- Workload visualization
- CSV export compatible with Maximo
- Basic reporting (PDF/Excel)

**Phase 2 - Costing & Reporting ($35,000)**
- Maintenance cost forecasting
- Budget vs actual tracking
- Executive dashboards
- Advanced custom reports

**Phase 3 - Integration & Automation ($45,000)**
- Maximo API connector (bidirectional)
- Automated alerts (email notifications)
- System scalability for future fleets

### 4.3 Risk Mitigation for Client

1. **Fixed Price** - No surprises, no overruns
2. **Phased Delivery** - Can stop after any phase with working software
3. **Code Ownership** - All source code delivered at each phase completion
4. **No Lock-in** - Documentation provided for any developer to maintain

---

## 5. ANSWERING THEIR QUESTIONS

### "Have you done this before?"
> "Yes. At MercadoLibre, I worked on logistics optimization that scheduled millions of deliveries across constraints like vehicle capacity, time windows, and driver availability. Your problem - scheduling maintenance across depot capacity, man-hours, and mileage limits - uses the same mathematical approach. The specific rules change, but the solution technique is proven."

### "Why should we trust a small company?"
> "Three reasons: First, you work directly with the person who builds it - no layers of project managers. Second, every phase delivers working software you own. Third, if we fail to deliver, you've lost one phase payment, not the whole project. The risk is contained."

### "What if the optimization doesn't work for our scale?"
> "The demo you'll see runs 128 trains over 52 weeks in under 3 seconds. That's your exact scale. The math scales linearly - 12 years is just more data, not a harder problem."

### "How does this work with Maximo?"
> "From Day 1, you can export the maintenance schedule as a CSV file and import it into Maximo. That's Level 1 integration - manual but functional. In Phase 3, we build the API connector so it syncs automatically. You don't have to wait for Phase 3 to use the system productively."

### "What exactly are we paying for in the first 3 weeks?"
> "The optimization engine. It's the most complex part of the system - the code that takes your 128 trains, your maintenance rules, your depot capacity, and produces an optimal schedule. By week 3, you can see it working with your constraints. The next 9 weeks of Phase 1 are building the user interface around that engine."

---

## 6. IMPLEMENTATION PLAN

### Week 1 (Dec 5-11): Presentation Development

| Day | Task | Deliverable |
|-----|------|-------------|
| Thu-Fri | Project setup, design system | Repo structure, styling |
| Sat-Sun | Static slides (hero вҶ’ approach) | 5 slides complete |
| Mon | Demo page with glpk.js | Working optimization |
| Tue | Proposal page + polish | Complete presentation |
| Wed | Deploy + test | Public URL ready |

### Week 2 (Dec 12+): Client Presentation

- Schedule call with Keolis
- Walk through presentation
- Live demo
- Answer questions
- Request decision timeline

### Technical Stack

| Component | Technology | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| Framework | Astro + React | Already in project, fast SSG |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Rapid development, professional look |
| Charts | Chart.js | Already installed, good animations |
| Solver | glpk.js | Client-side MIP, no backend needed |
| Hosting | Vercel | Free tier, instant deploy |

---

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

### Presentation Must:
- [ ] Load in under 2 seconds
- [ ] Work on mobile (they may view on phone)
- [ ] Demo complete optimization in under 5 seconds
- [ ] Show before/after with their numbers (128 trains, 12 years)
- [ ] Have zero technical jargon visible to client
- [ ] Include downloadable PDF of revised proposal

### After Presentation, Client Should:
- [ ] Understand we've done this before
- [ ] See the system working with their parameters
- [ ] Have clear pricing with no surprises
- [ ] Know exactly what each phase delivers
- [ ] Feel confident we can deliver

---

## 8. RISK MATRIX

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| glpk.js too slow for demo | Low | High | Pre-compute fallback, smaller demo set |
| Client wants more features | Medium | Medium | "Phase 2/3 scope, happy to discuss" |
| They're talking to competitors | High | High | Speed to demo, show working code |
| Technical questions we can't answer | Low | Medium | Honest: "I'll research and follow up" |
| They want to negotiate price | Medium | Low | Price is fair, willing to discuss scope |

---

## 9. FILES TO CREATE

```
apps/presentation/           вҶ’ New Astro app
в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ pages/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ index.astro     вҶ’ Hero slide
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ challenge.astro вҶ’ Problem visualization
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ solution.astro  вҶ’ Solution explanation
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ demo.astro      вҶ’ Interactive demo
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ approach.astro  вҶ’ Methodology/phases
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ experience.astroвҶ’ Why us
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ proposal.astro  вҶ’ Commercial terms
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ next.astro      вҶ’ Call to action
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ components/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Navigation.tsx  вҶ’ Slide navigation
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ WorkloadChart.tsx вҶ’ Before/after charts
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Optimizer.tsx   вҶ’ glpk.js integration
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ PhaseTimeline.tsx вҶ’ Visual timeline
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ lib/
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ solver.ts       вҶ’ Optimization logic
в””в”Җв”Җ public/
    в””в”Җв”Җ proposal.pdf        вҶ’ Downloadable SoW
```

---

## 10. NEXT IMMEDIATE ACTION

**START HERE:** Create `apps/presentation` with basic structure and navigation.

The presentation is the deliverable. Everything else is preparation for it.

---

*End of Plan*

# Project Briefing: Dubai Metro MMS

**Classification**: CONFIDENTIAL
**Last Updated**: 2025-12-07
**Original**: PROJECT.pdf (converted to markdown)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

### The Opportunity

Software development contract for a **Mileage Management System (MMS)** for Dubai Metro.
Uses optimization to schedule preventive maintenance for 128 trains over a 12-year planning horizon.

### Key Facts

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Client** | Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro Operator) |
| **Contract Value** | Up to $147,500 USD |
| **Fleet Size** | 128 trains (79 Alstom + 49 Kinki Sharyo) |
| **Planning Horizon** | 12 years (624 weeks) |
| **Phase 0** | $12,500 (Discovery) |

### The Problem We Solve

Without optimization, maintenance events cluster together creating:
- **Resource bottlenecks** - too many trains needing maintenance at once
- **Underutilization** - wasted capacity on slow weeks
- **Compliance risks** - trains exceeding mileage limits
- **Unpredictable budgets** - no long-term visibility

Our solution levels the workload, reducing peaks by 40%+ and ensuring predictable resource utilization.

---

## 2. The Client: Keolis MHI

Joint venture operating Dubai Metro under a 15-year contract with Dubai's Roads & Transport Authority (RTA), starting September 2021.

| Asset | Details |

| Name | Role | Email |
|------|------|-------|

| Anase Kunhapputty | Technical Team | KM_Anase.Kunhapputty@dubaimetro.ae |

---

## 3. The Problem

### Current Situation: Manual Planning in Excel

128 trains Г— 12 years Г— 5+ maintenance types = **manually unmanageable**.

Problems:
- Resource bottlenecks (peaks overwhelming workshop)
- Underutilization (wasted capacity)
- Compliance risk (trains exceeding mileage limits)
- No long-term visibility
- Operations disconnect from maintenance planning

|--------|-------|--------|

| Maintenance types | ~5 per train type | ~640+ events |
| Planning horizon | 12 years = 4,380 days | Massive timeline |
| Flexibility window | Вұ15 days per event | ~30 options each |
| **Possible schedules** | 30^640 вүҲ 10^946 | Computationally intractable |

This is why we use mathematical optimization - not exhaustive enumeration.

---

## 4. Our Solution: Mileage Management System

### Three Capabilities

| Capability | Horizon | Key Functions |
|------------|---------|---------------|
| Strategic Planning | 1-12 years | Forecast maintenance, optimize schedule, simulations |
| Tactical Operations | Daily | Track actual vs. planned, RUC recommendations, alerts |
| Resource Management | Continuous | Track capacity, forecast costs, reports |

### The RUC System

**RUC = Recommended Usage Category**

Each day, every train gets classified based on how its actual mileage compares to plan:

| Category | Condition | Action |
|----------|-----------|--------|
| **ACCELERATE** | Actual < Planned | Use more, longer routes |
| **NOMINAL** | Actual вүҲ Planned | Normal assignment |
| **DECELERATE** | Actual > Planned | Use less, shorter routes |

This creates a feedback loop that keeps actual mileage aligned with the optimized plan.

### Deliverables by Phase

| Phase | Price | Duration | Deliverable |
|-------|-------|----------|-------------|
| 0: Discovery | $12,500 | 3 weeks | Optimization Playground |
| 1: MVP | $55,000 NTE | 12 weeks | Working system with core features |
| 2: Costing | $35,000 NTE | 6 weeks | Financial module and reports |
| 3: Integration | $45,000 NTE | 8 weeks | Maximo integration and alerts |

**NTE = Not To Exceed** (guaranteed maximum price)

---

## 5. Technical Architecture

> **Note**: This describes the **proposed** architecture. See `SOLVER_ARCHITECTURE_DECISION.md` for current implementation status.

### Proposed Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|-------|------------|-----------|
| Runtime | Bun.js | Fast, native TypeScript |
| Frontend | Astro + React | Static + islands |
| API | Hono (on Bun) | Minimal, fast |
| ORM | Drizzle | Type-safe, lightweight |
| Solver | **TBD** - see decision doc | MIP vs Heuristic |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16 | ACID, analytical queries |

### Solver Architecture Decision

The original proposal described Python + HiGHS MIP solver.
Current implementation uses JS heuristic for Phase 0 demos.

**Decision pending**: See `docs/internal/SOLVER_ARCHITECTURE_DECISION.md`

---

## 6. Commercial Positioning

### Market Position

| Vendor Type | Price Range |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Our Proposal** | $147,500 (fixed max) |
| Indian IT Services | $80k - $150k |
| European Specialists | $200k - $500k |
| Enterprise (SAP, IBM) | $500k - $2M+ |

### Why Competitive Pricing?

- No enterprise overhead
- Same solver technology used by Google (HiGHS is MIT licensed)
- Direct access to builders, no support tiers
- NTE structure = client bears no overrun risk

---

## 7. Negotiation History

| Date | Event | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Oct 8, 2025 | RFI Received | Initial contact from procurement |
| Oct 9, 2025 | Intent Confirmed | Expressed interest |
| Oct 23, 2025 | Technical Call | Presented capabilities |
| Nov 7, 2025 | NDA Requested | Confidentiality required |
| Nov 10, 2025 | NDA Signed | Gained access to scope |
| Nov 11, 2025 | RFQ Received | Full requirements |
| Nov 18, 2025 | SOW Submitted | Proposal sent |
| Dec 3, 2025 | Meeting Request | Positive signal |
| Dec 4, 2025 | **Meeting Held** | Company intro, scope discussion |

---

## 8. Competitive Analysis

### What Major Metros Use

| System Type | Examples | What It Does | What It Doesn't Do |
|-------------|----------|--------------|-------------------|
| CMMS/EAM | IBM Maximo, SAP PM | Work orders, inventory | Mathematical optimization |
| Excel/Manual | Most operators | Ad-hoc planning | Optimization, forecasting |
| PdM Platforms | Alstom HealthHub | Sensor data, ML prediction | Schedule optimization |
| OR Solutions | Custom, rare | Mathematical optimization | Usually not productized |

**Key Insight**: Maximo is industry standard for asset management but does NOT include mathematical optimization. We complement Maximo, not compete with it.

### Solver Options

| Solver | Type | License | Performance |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| Gurobi | Commercial | $30k+/year | Best |
| CPLEX | Commercial | $30k+/year | Best |
| HiGHS | Open Source | MIT | ~10x slower, but free |
| OR-Tools | Open Source | Apache 2.0 | Good for CP |

HiGHS is defensible for a $150k project. Phase 0 validates if it's sufficient.

---

## 9. Strengths and Gaps

### Strengths

- Mathematical optimization is correct technique
- Strategic/tactical split is standard architecture
- Phased approach validates before commitment
- Complements Maximo (fills gap)
- Cost-effective (90% cheaper than enterprise)

### Gaps / Risks

| Gap | Mitigation |
|-----|------------|
| No Predictive Maintenance (IoT/ML) | Different layer, not in RFQ scope |
| Solver performance unvalidated | Phase 0 benchmarks this |
| Small company risk | Phased approach, standalone phases |
| No railway-specific experience | Math is domain-agnostic |

---

## 10. Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| PM | Preventive Maintenance (mileage or time-based) |
| MIP | Mixed-Integer Programming (optimization technique) |
| HiGHS | Open-source optimization solver |
| RUC | Recommended Usage Category (ACCELERATE/NOMINAL/DECELERATE) |
| Drift | Deviation between actual and planned mileage |
| Master Plan | Optimized long-term maintenance schedule |
| Resource Leveling | Distributing workload evenly over time |
| NTE | Not To Exceed (guaranteed maximum price) |
| CMMS | Computerized Maintenance Management System |
| EAM | Enterprise Asset Management |

---

## Confidential Section

### How We Got This Opportunity

Professional referral through a contact at Keolis MHI who had previously engaged ZAZCART for web development work.

**Meeting Protocol**:
- Treat all Keolis employees professionally
- Do not reference internal connections
- Focus on technical capabilities and value proposition

### Company Information

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Legal Entity | ZAZCART LTDA |
| CNPJ | 25.350.064/0001-43 |
| Location | SГЈo Paulo, Brazil |
| Founded | 2016 |
| Contact | antonio@zazcart.com |

---

# Session Summary - December 8, 2025

**Duration:** ~2 hours
**Focus:** Database Layer Migration (Step 1 of Demo Week)

---

## What Was Accomplished

### 1. packages/db Created

- Created new `packages/db/` directory
- Copied document-client code from SM project
- Set up package.json with all dependencies
- Configured vitest for testing

### 2. Dependencies Installed

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@sinclair/typebox": "^0.34.14",
    "@sinclair/typebox-codegen": "^0.11.1",
    "big.js": "^6.2.2",
    "dataloader": "^2.2.3",
    "mongodb": "^6.17.0",
    "powership": "^5.8.8",
    "sift": "^17.1.3"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "conditional-type-checks": "^1.0.6",
    "mongodb-memory-server": "^10.1.4",
    "vitest": "^3.2.0"
  }
}
```

### 3. Import Paths Fixed

- Changed `@utils/type-check.ts` вҶ’ `../utils/type-check.ts` in:
  - entity/entity.ts
  - entity/entity-loader.ts
  - query-types.ts
  - document-index.ts

### 4. Test Results

```
68 passed, 3 failed, 37 skipped (108 total)

Passing:
вң“ conust/conust.spec.ts (37 tests) - Index encoding
вң“ document-index.test.ts (2 tests)
вң“ document-client-02.spec.ts (24 tests)
вң“ entity.test.ts (4 tests)

Failing/Skipped:
вң— entity-validations.test.ts - Codegen.ModelToMongoDBSchema.Generate undefined
вң— document-client.test.ts - MONGO_URI undefined
вң— document-client-advanced.spec.ts - MONGO_URI undefined
вң— entity-schema-validation.test.ts - MONGO_URI undefined
```

---

## Known Issues to Fix

### Issue 1: typebox-codegen API Mismatch

**Problem:** `Codegen.ModelToMongoDBSchema.Generate` is undefined
**Cause:** SM project uses `workspace:*` version which may have custom modifications
**Location:** `packages/db/src/document-client/entity/entity.ts:226`
**Solution Options:**

1. Check exact version in SM's monorepo and match it
2. Copy SM's local typebox-codegen package
3. Temporarily skip setupDatabase() calls in tests

### Issue 3: TypeBox Migration Consideration

**Question:** Should we migrate to new TypeBox without namespace?
**Context:**

- SM project uses older TypeBox with `@sinclair/typebox` namespace
- Newer TypeBox (0.30+) removed namespace pattern
- All entity code uses: `import { Type, TObject } from '@sinclair/typebox'`

**Decision to Make:**

1. Keep current version (match SM exactly)
2. Migrate to new version (requires code changes throughout document-client)

**Recommendation:** For demo week, keep current version to minimize risk.
Migration can be done post-demo as part of Phase 1 cleanup.

### Issue 2: Test Isolation (ormMock)

**Problem:** `process.env.MONGO_URI` is undefined when tests import `ormMock`
**Cause:** `ormMock.ts` reads env var at import time, but `mongodb.mock.ts` sets it in `beforeAll`
**Solution Options:**

1. Make ormMock lazy (function that reads env var on call, not import)
2. Use vitest `setupFilesAfterEnv` to ensure mongodb.mock.ts runs first
3. Modify tests to not import ormMock at top level

---

## TODO.md Updated With:

1. New "NEXT SESSION - IMMEDIATE ACTIONS" section
2. Detailed Step 1 progress with test results
3. Known issues documented
4. Demo strategy section (impress, don't hide)
5. SM document-client migration status

## TECHNICAL_DEBT.md Updated With:

1. DATA-001 status now "IN PROGRESS"
2. Detailed sub-tasks for database implementation
3. Session progress section

---

## Files Created/Modified

### Created:

- `packages/db/package.json`
- `packages/db/tsconfig.json`
- `packages/db/vitest.config.ts`
- `packages/db/src/index.ts`
- `packages/db/src/utils/index.ts`
- `packages/db/src/utils/type-check.ts`
- `packages/db/src/document-client/` (all files from SM)
- `packages/db/src/__mock__/` (mongodb.mock.ts, ormMock.ts, etc)

### Modified:

- `TODO.md` - major update with progress and next steps
- `docs/TECHNICAL_DEBT.md` - DATA-001 progress

---

## Demo Strategy Documented

**Core Principle:** IMPRESS, don't hide.

- Keep ALL buttons visible
- Fill dashboards with metrics
- Use "AI-Optimized" badges
- Show scenario history
- Premium loading animations
- Never apologize for scope

Non-functional features must be:

1. Registered for implementation
2. Evaluated for quick wins
3. Implemented if strategic

---

## Next Session Priority

1. Fix typebox-codegen API issue
2. Fix test isolation for ormMock
3. Get 108/108 tests passing
4. Create MMS entity schemas (Train, MaintenanceType, etc)
5. Create seed script (128 trains)
6. Start API integration (Step 2)

---

## Pending Decision: TypeBox Migration

**Question from user:** "Precisamos verificar se compensa migrar para o novo package 'typebox' (sem namespace)"

**Context:**

- New TypeBox (v0.30+) removed the namespace pattern
- Current SM code uses namespace pattern: `import { Type } from '@sinclair/typebox'`
- This still works in newer versions, but some sub-packages may have changed

**Factors to Consider:**

1. **Time risk** - Migration during demo week is risky
2. **Breaking changes** - Need to verify what changed between versions
3. **typebox-codegen compatibility** - The codegen issue may be related to version mismatch
4. **Long-term maintenance** - Newer version may be better maintained

**Recommendation:**

- For demo week: Match SM's exact version (use workspace package if needed)
- Post-demo (Phase 1): Evaluate migration with proper testing

**To investigate next session:**

```bash
# Check SM monorepo structure for local packages
ls -la /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/sm/packages/

# Check if typebox or typebox-codegen are local
find /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/sm -name "package.json" -exec grep -l "typebox" {} \;
```

1вҶ’# Solver Architecture Decision
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Status**: PENDING DECISION
     4вҶ’**Date**: 2025-12-07
     5вҶ’**Impact**: Critical - affects 12-year project scalability
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’---
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’## Context
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’The MMS needs a solver to optimize maintenance schedules for 128 trains over 12 years.
    12вҶ’Two approaches were considered:
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’| Approach | Implementation | Status |
    15вҶ’|----------|---------------|--------|
    16вҶ’| **A) JS Heuristic** | `packages/solver/` | Built (current) |
    17вҶ’| **B) Python MIP** | Not implemented | Proposed in SOW |
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’---
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’## Current State: JS Heuristic Solver
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’**What exists**: A TypeScript heuristic solver using greedy assignment with iterative improvement.
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’**Performance**:
    26вҶ’- 12-year horizon (624 weeks): **2.58 seconds**
    27вҶ’- 20/20 tests passing
    28вҶ’- ~75% mileage compliance
    29вҶ’- Resource leveling within acceptable bounds
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’**Limitations**:
    32вҶ’- **Not mathematically optimal** - finds "good enough" solutions
    33вҶ’- No guarantee of finding the best schedule
    34вҶ’- May miss optimization opportunities that compound over 12 years
    35вҶ’- Less defensible in technical due diligence
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’---
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’## Proposed: Python MIP Solver (HiGHS)
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’**What it provides**:
    42вҶ’- **Mathematically proven** optimal or near-optimal solutions
    43вҶ’- Industry standard for railway scheduling (see RESEARCH_MAINTENANCE_SCHEDULING.md)
    44вҶ’- Used by: Tokyo Metro, Deutsche Bahn, SNCF, airlines
    45вҶ’- Academically validated approach
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’**Architecture**:
    48вҶ’```
    49вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    50вҶ’в”Ӯ                    TypeScript API                        в”Ӯ
    51вҶ’в”Ӯ                    (Bun.js + Hono)                       в”Ӯ

   124вҶ’### Step 3: Integration

   129вҶ’### Step 4: Validation

   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’---

   136вҶ’## Risk Assessment

   146вҶ’
   147вҶ’## Decision Required
   148вҶ’
   149вҶ’**Options**:
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’1. **Keep JS Heuristic Only** - simpler, faster, "good enough"
   152вҶ’2. **Implement Python MIP Only** - academically correct, slower
   153вҶ’3. **Hybrid Approach** - best of both, more complex
   154вҶ’

   156вҶ’
   157вҶ’- Phase 0: JS heuristic (already done)
   158вҶ’- Phase 1+: Python MIP for production, JS as fallback
